---
title: "Lowering IR to a model instance"
subtitle: "T2 · Phase T2.2 · `va-codegen::lower`"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: code complete.** Generated resistor/diode reproduce `va-abi`'s own hand-checked
reference stamps exactly; the §5 AD-vs-FD milestone is green. Historically (2026-07-14, against
the smaller 62-file frontend baseline that predates the corpus growing to 150 tracked files),
50/62 frontend-passing files also passed codegen — not re-measured against the current
114-passing frontend, so treat that ratio as a snapshot, not a live number.
:::

## Goal

Turn an elaborated `va_ir::Module` into a real `Box<dyn ModelInstance>` — something `va-core`
can actually call `load` on — using the AD core (`t2-codegen/01-ad-core.qmd`) to compute exact
stamps.

## Where it fits

```
 va_ir::Module ──► [build_instance] ──► GeneratedModel (impl ModelInstance) ──► va-core
                         │
                    lower.rs: flatten contributions/control flow into a stampable form
```

## The idea

**Flow contributions split into resistive/charge terms up front.** `lower::lower` walks the
module's `analog` block once and produces a `Lowered` structure: every `<+` contribution is
classified into its resistive part (stamped every solve) and, if it involves `ddt`, its charge
part (stamped only when a transient companion model differentiates it in time). DC analysis
simply never looks at the charge channel.

**`if`/`else` genuinely can't be flattened away** the way `+`/`-` can — which arm runs depends on
`x`, so a naive "evaluate everything, pick the answer" approach would evaluate an arm that might
be undefined at the current point. `LoweredStmt::If { cond, then_, else_ }` carries *both* arms
as their own lowered statement sequences; `GeneratedModel::run` (which `load` calls) picks one at
evaluation time based on the condition, the same rule `ad::eval`'s `Select` already follows.

**Eager, both-arms validation, still with a single-taken-arm runtime.** `build_instance` must
validate that a module is fully within the v0-supported subset *before* returning it — so that
`ModelInstance::load` can never fail later, deep inside a Newton iteration. A model does that
validation once at construction, at the all-zero point. But if validation only walked whichever
`if` arm is taken *at that one point*, an unsupported construct hiding in the *other* arm would
slip through and only surface much later, at a different operating point mid-solve.
`GeneratedModel::validate`/`validate_stmts` therefore walk **both** arms of every `if`
unconditionally — an honest over-approximation (sound for the common "both arms assign the same
variables" compact-model shape; not full path-sensitive analysis) — while `run` itself still only
ever evaluates the one arm the current `x` actually selects. A regression test builds a module
whose *unselected* arm at `x=0` contains an unassigned-variable read, and asserts
`build_instance` still rejects it eagerly, not just on a later `load` call at a different point.

## The code

```rust
pub fn build_instance(
    module: &Module,
    terminals: &[usize],       // one global unknown per module.nodes entry, in node order
    next_unknown: &mut usize,  // caller's free-index counter; advanced for every extra unknown
) -> Result<Box<dyn ModelInstance>, CodegenError>
```

`build_instance` allocates one further global unknown per branch that has its own auxiliary
current (a potential contribution that isn't a simple two-terminal stamp) and one per distinct
`idt(...)` call site — `lowered.n_unknowns` is the authoritative total, so a caller (`va-cli`'s
device-building loop) never has to pre-compute how many extra unknowns a module needs; it just
keeps calling `build_instance` with its own running counter.

```rust
struct GeneratedModel {
    module: Module,        // kept for its arena — ad::eval reads expressions straight from it
    terminals: Vec<usize>, // full unknown list: node terminals + auxiliary unknowns
    params: Vec<f64>,      // resolved defaults, first entry overridable by a netlist device value
    lowered: lower::Lowered,
    vt: f64,
    temp: f64,
}

impl ModelInstance for GeneratedModel {
    fn load(&self, x: &[f64], sink: &mut dyn StampSink) { /* runs self.lowered via ad::eval */ }
}
```

## It works

```console
$ cargo test -p va-codegen
test result: ok. (generated resistor/diode match va-abi's own hand-checked stamps; the
if/else branch-selection test asserts both the stamped residual *and* Jacobian differ correctly
across the two arms, proving the selected-arm-only runtime works; the eager-both-arms-validation
regression test above)
```

## Limitations

Still a real subset, not full Verilog-A codegen: `while`/`for`/`repeat`/`case` and user-defined
analog functions are elaborated into `va-ir` (T1.3) but `build_instance` still rejects them
during its own lowering — a corpus file that only *elaborates* successfully doesn't necessarily
*codegen*, a distinct, narrower bar (§ this tutorial's own status callout). Local-variable reads
before assignment are also rejected rather than defaulting to some value. As with T1.3, none of
this is hidden: `build_instance`'s eager validation reports exactly which construct blocked a
given file, rather than silently miscompiling it.

## What's next

`t2-codegen/03-charge-and-coverage.qmd` — the charge channel (`ddt`) and how corpus coverage is
tracked going forward.
